kept.text:
I love my mining stocks but this is literally a bad and misleading take. $AMZN Amazon has a giant moat and a toe hold into the biggest consumer the world has ever seen. They also have a strong media franchise, a near flawless distribution hub and logistics channel, an AWS system that is critical and sticky to enterprise clients, is a big part of the AI boom, and by the way they make $50b a year in revenue from subscriptions alone! They’ve spent massive amounts of capex recently to continue investing in and growing their business that will likely pay a higher return on capital. They have a near monopoly, really. 

To compare relative valuations in FCF or whatever metric you want to draw up, it’s🍎 and 🍊and a massive stretch. Amazon has pricing power. $CDE and all other mining companies don’t, they are price takers and live or die by the cyclical nature of the underlying commodity price. Also mining stocks have finite resources and must continuously spend on capex to keep said resources from declining. Stocks like these trade on NAV, mine life multiples, and commodity cycles, not perpetual earnings growth assumptions in a volatile cyclical market. Amazon is infinite. CDE would never trade beyond a reasonable multiple of its mine life, nor would any other miner period. 

Stick to comparing relative valuations within same sector and with similar situated mining stock peer group and good management. That’s where you find the edge. I can’t believe I just read this.

kept.text:
$AMZN is just an absolute beast of a company.

The more I look at the business, the more impressive it gets.

Just look at the revenue growth.

Amazon went from $136B in annual revenue in 2016 to nearly $717B in 2025.

That is over 5x bigger in less than a decade.

And the crazy part is this is not some one-trick company.

Amazon has:
AWS
Advertising
E-commerce
Prime
Third-party seller services
Logistics
Devices
AI infrastructure

That is what makes the company so powerful.

It has multiple massive businesses all feeding into each other.

Most companies would be lucky to build one great business.

Amazon built an entire ecosystem.

That is why I think so many people underestimate it.

It is one of the most dominant and diversified businesses in the world.

Still one of my highest conviction long-term holdings.

The Pentagon has signed agreements with Nvidia, Microsoft, Reflection AI and Amazon Web Services to deploy advanced AI tools on classified networks, expanding a roster that includes SpaceX, OpenAI and Google. Officials confirmed the arrangements anonymously. AWS said it will continue supporting military modernization, while Microsoft declined comment and Nvidia and Reflection did not respond.
